description: In C, validate the argv parsing, fread/fgets bounds, and ntohl/htonl on wire data where external data crosses in.
body: In C, defects cluster at argv parsing, fread/fgets bounds, and ntohl/htonl on wire data, where assumptions about types, ownership, or encoding stop holding. Use when a change crosses header guards, the ABI of exported symbols, and struct layout/padding or consumes data from outside the program. Do not assume the other side honors your contract, and avoid trusting input without checking it. Actions: identify the boundary, test both valid and malformed input, verify the error path, and record the assumptions you are relying on. Validation signals include tests for the failure path, a trace of rejected input, and a documented contract at header guards, the ABI of exported symbols, and struct layout/padding. Risk boundary: escalate when the boundary handles untrusted input, crosses a trust/security line, or feeds a system you cannot roll back.
